title: Rate of child deaths attributed to child and maternal malnutrition
subtitle: The estimated number of deaths attributed to child and maternal malnutrition, per 100,000 children under the age of 5.
caption:
Rate of child deaths attributed to child and maternal malnutrition

The estimated number of deaths attributed to child and maternal malnutrition, per 100,000 children under the age of 5.

This choropleth map displays the estimated rate of child deaths attributed to child and maternal malnutrition per 100,000 children under the age of 5 for the year 2023. The data is visualized using a color gradient ranging from light beige for the lowest rates to dark maroon for the highest, with a legend scale extending from 0 to 2,000. The highest concentration of mortality rates is visible across Sub-Saharan Africa, where a large cluster of countries including Chad, the Central African Republic, and Somalia are shaded in the darkest maroon bands, indicating values exceeding 500 and reaching into the 1,000 to 2,000 range. Surrounding nations in West and East Africa generally fall within the 200 to 500 band. In Asia, Afghanistan and Yemen appear in the darker orange to red bands, suggesting rates between 100 and 500, while India and Pakistan sit in the intermediate orange ranges roughly between 50 and 200. Most of South America, including Brazil and Argentina, along with Mexico, are colored in lighter orange shades corresponding to the 20 to 50 band. North America, Europe, Australia, and Russia are predominantly shaded in the lightest beige, representing rates between 0 and 10. Greenland and a few other territories are hatched, denoting areas with no available data. The visual distribution highlights a distinct geographic disparity, with the most intense coloration concentrated in the central band of the African continent and scattered pockets in South Asia and the Middle East.
